_note: Question bank for the diary prompt loop. Mix of practice/craft reflection + interest-specific prompts (modular synthesis, TouchDesigner, generative work, AI collaboration, Toronto, independent-artist economics, audiovisual performance). Add more freely — the picker reads them all and avoids recent repeats.
questions: What did you make this week that you'd want to see again in a year?
Where did you compromise today between what looked right and what felt right?
Name one thing you understood today that you didn't yesterday.
What problem did you solve by waiting?
Which tool surprised you with its limits this week?
What's the smallest thing you could ship that would still be meaningful?
Where did you over-engineer something today?
What did you almost delete that turned out to be the best part?
When was the last time you watched something work the first time? What was different?
What feels finished but isn't?
What feels unfinished but actually is?
What did you avoid this week that you knew you should do?
What's the question behind the question you've been asking yourself?
Where are you imitating instead of building?
What constraint helped you most this week?
Name a small thing you did today that future-you will thank you for.
Which idea has shown up in your work three times this month?
What's the difference between resting and stalling for you right now?
What's one thing you can stop doing tomorrow?
Which collaborator (human, AI, or tool) gave you the most this week?
What part of your practice has been on autopilot?
Where did craft beat speed today?
Where did speed beat craft today?
What would you build if no one was going to see it?
What would change if the deadline doubled?
What would change if the deadline halved?
What problem keeps coming back? What's the root?
Which idea did you have today that you wouldn't have had last year?
What did someone teach you today, intentionally or not?
Where is the friction you're avoiding telling you something important?
What's the bravest thing you did this week, however small?
What's the most boring task you did today, and why does it matter?
Where did taste win over technique?
Where did technique win over taste?
What's a piece of your work that's aged better than you expected?
What's a piece of your work that's aged worse than you expected?
Who would you make this for if you could pick one person?
What's the thing you keep wanting to start? What's stopping you?
Where did playfulness sneak into your work this week?
What did you learn by failing today?
What's a constraint you're treating as a problem but is actually a feature?
Which tool would you replace tomorrow if you had time?
Which tool would you keep no matter what?
What feedback did you ignore this week, rightly or wrongly?
What feedback did you take this week that changed something?
Where are you measuring the wrong thing?
What's the simplest version of this that still has soul?
Which decision did you delay today? Why?
What does momentum feel like for you right now?
What's worth doing badly this week?
Which synth in your current rig has the highest decision-to-output ratio? Why is the one you reach for first not always that one?
What's a patch you can't reproduce that you wish you could? What would make it less precious to lose?
Where is your modular system smarter than you? Where are you smarter than it?
Which module justifies its HP, and which is real estate you're paying for in nostalgia?
What did the Octatrack teach you about composition that no DAW did?
What's a sound you've been chasing for years? What does it actually represent to you?
When you record a live take, what mistakes do you keep and why?
Where in your signal chain are you adding character vs. correcting?
What's the difference between a happy accident and a habit you've stopped questioning?
If you had to delete half your sample library to make better use of the rest, which half stays?
What track did you nearly finish and then ruin by adding one more thing?
Which BPM feels like home? What does that say about your nervous system?
What's a chord progression you keep returning to without meaning to?
Where does your music live in the gap between the kit you have and the kit you want?
What's the difference between hearing and listening when you're producing?
What's a track from your past that you couldn't make today, even with the same gear?
What would you sacrifice for one more good take?
When did your gear stop being the bottleneck?
What do you mix for: the loud speakers, the laptop, or the headphones at 2am?
What's a song you wrote that the listener will never know was a love letter to a synth?
Where is repetition serving you in your music, and where is it hiding from growth?
If you could only use one Eurorack module for a year, which is it and what does that say?
What sound have you avoided learning to make? Why?
Where does silence do more work than sound in your tracks?
What's a Eurorack patch you've abandoned that wanted to be a song?
Which Ableton workflow saves you minutes daily — and which costs you them?
When did you last commit to a take instead of resampling it?
What's the difference between a sketch and a song for you right now?
When was the last time you finished a track because you ran out of options vs. because you found the right one?
What's the role of the Reel-to-Reel in your process — sound, ritual, or both?
Which classic break do you secretly want to retire? Which one will you never tire of?
When does sequencing feel like writing versus assembling?
What does mastering mean to you — translation, polish, or surrender?
What's an unfinished album in your head? What's the actual blocker?
If you could only release one track this year, what makes you choose it?
What's the difference between a banger and a track you'd live with for years?
Where in your process do you trust your ears the least?
What does your favorite mix engineer hear that you don't?
If a non-musician sat with you for an hour in the studio, what would you want them to take away?
What does your studio look like when you're stuck? What does it look like when you're flowing?
What's a sample you can't license but can't unhear?
Which knob do you reach for unconsciously? What does that reveal?
What's a TouchDesigner network from this week that wants to become a tool you reuse, not a one-off?
Which POP node taught you something about geometry you didn't know before?
When did a node tree make you laugh out loud at how elegant it became?
What's a TD project you've started three times and never shipped? What's the real reason?
Where are you fighting TD instead of working with it?
What's the simplest TD network you've built that does the most work?
When does TD feel like instrument vs. canvas vs. spreadsheet for you?
What's an audio-reactive concept you've avoided because the math felt opaque?
When did you last reverse-engineer a TouchDesigner patch someone else made? What did you learn beyond the technique?
What's a visual you can't quite get out of your head? What's stopping you from making it?
Which shader from this week would you frame on a wall if it were a still?
What's a generative system you've built that runs unattended and still surprises you?
Where did randomness add depth, and where did it add noise?
What's the difference between a generative piece and a procedural one in your work?
Which p5.js or three.js sketch from your archive deserves another pass?
What's a particle system that almost worked? What's the one parameter away?
When did GLSL stop feeling like a language and start feeling like a paintbrush?
What's the trade-off in your shaders between performance and beauty right now?
What's a constraint you put on a visual system that made it better?
What would a 30-second loop of your best week look like as a visual?
Where in your generative work are you using AI to bypass learning vs. accelerate it?
Which video you shot this year underestimates how good it is?
What's the difference between a VJ set and a film for you?
What's a piece of footage from a year ago you didn't know what to do with that makes sense now?
Where in your editing do you cut for rhythm, and where for narrative?
Which DaVinci Resolve trick changed your workflow most this year?
What's the difference between color grading and color shaping in your eye?
When did Unreal click for you, or hasn't it yet?
What's a real-time visual that you'd rather have rendered offline? Why did you go real-time anyway?
What did you try to make Claude do this week that surprised you in the result — good or bad?
Where did Claude make a decision you wouldn't have, and was it better?
What's something you tried to prompt that revealed your own confusion about what you wanted?
When did AI accelerate you past a wall you'd been stuck on for months?
When did AI distract you from a wall you needed to push through yourself?
What's the difference between collaborating with Claude and using Claude?
What did Claude write or build today that you could not have? What does that change?
What did you ask Claude that you should have figured out yourself? Was that a good trade?
What's a skill you no longer practice because AI does it for you? Is that loss real or hypothetical?
Where in your work is the AI's voice creeping into yours? Where is yours staying yours?
If you couldn't use Claude tomorrow, what would you build first?
What's the most valuable thing AI has given you this month — code, time, courage, or something else?
Where did you trust Claude more than you trust yourself? Was that wise?
What did AI not understand this week that a friend would have?
What does your dream Claude integration look like for your music or visuals?
When did an AI failure teach you something about your assumptions?
What's a workflow you'd never share publicly because it'd look like cheating? Is it?
What's the difference between AI as tool and AI as audience?
What would your work look like if you'd had Claude five years ago?
What does Claude do that you used to pay other humans for? How do you feel about that?
What's a Toronto sound or look you can identify in 5 seconds? What makes it that way?
Which Toronto venue holds a memory you wish more people knew about?
What does the city contribute to your work that nowhere else could?
What's a Toronto creative habit you've absorbed without noticing?
Which Toronto artist do you wish you'd collaborated with five years ago?
What's a Toronto-specific constraint that shapes your art (weather, rent, transit, anything)?
When did Toronto's scene give you a thing, and when did it take a thing?
What's a neighborhood here that sounds like a track you'd make?
What would change about your work if you moved to Berlin? To Tokyo? To a small Ontario town?
What does it mean to be a Toronto artist on the internet?
Which Toronto institution (gallery, school, label) has earned your trust over time? Why?
If a curator from another city visited you for a day, where would you take them?
What does TIFF or Nuit Blanche unlock for you, or what do you avoid about them?
What's a Toronto venue you'd build if you had the resources?
What does the MUTEK lineup tell you about where music is heading?
If a stranger landed on wavgen.ca for 30 seconds, what one truth about your work do you want them to leave with?
What's a section of wavgen.ca you keep meaning to rewrite? What's the real blocker?
When you imagine wavgen.ca in five years, what's still there? What's gone?
Which page on wavgen.ca makes you proud? Which one makes you wince?
What's a feature you built that nobody uses? Should it stay?
What does wavgen.ca do that no other artist site does?
If wavgen.ca had to lose half its content, which half would you keep?
Where on the site do you sound like yourself, and where do you sound like a portfolio template?
What would change if you added a guestbook or comments?
What does the site teach you about how you think about your work?
Which automation on wavgen.ca pays the best dividend per minute of setup?
If wavgen.ca had a print edition, what would be in it?
What's a constraint you've put on the site (no analytics, no ads, etc.) that you'd defend out loud?
Where does the site reveal what you actually care about vs. what you think you should care about?
What's the difference between performing for 5 people and 50 for you? Where's the inflection point?
What's a moment in a live set when you knew everything had clicked?
What's a moment in a live set when you knew you'd lost the room — and what did you try?
Which gear breaks most often in your live rig? Why do you keep it?
What does soundcheck reveal about a venue's character?
When did silence between songs work harder than the songs?
What's your relationship with the click track in live performance?

What did you put off until it solved itself? Was that wisdom or avoidance?
What does morning-you and evening-you disagree about?
